Title: A NIGT1-centred transcriptional cascade regulates nitrate signalling and incorporates phosphorus starvation signals in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major nitrogen source for synthesis of amino acids, nucleotides and chlorophyll
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NRT2.1 expression is repressed by NIGT1 proteins and downregulated under phosphorus starvation, reducing nitrate uptake activity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root-expressed, nitrate-inducible; transiently induced after nitrate supply
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-expressed, nitrate-inducible; transiently induced after nitrate supply
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter-LUC co-transfection assays; EMSA on promoter cis-elements; ChIP; transgenic promoter mutagenesis with in vivo LUC imaging; RT-qPCR; 15NO3- uptake assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate supply; nitrogen starvation; high nitrate; phosphorus starvation
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native NRT2.1 promoter; mutated NRT2.1 promoter with disrupted NIGT1-binding sites
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NRT2.1 is a direct repression target of NIGT1.1 through two proximal GAATC motifs in its promoter. 2. NLP7 activates NRT2.1 independently through distinct cis-elements, while NIGT1 antagonizes this activation. 3. Under phosphorus starvation, the PHR1-NIGT1 cascade contributes to NRT2.1 repression and reduced nitrate uptake.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modifying NIGT1–NRT2.1 regulation may improve nitrate uptake and nitrogen-use efficiency, especially under fluctuating N and P conditions.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering repression may disturb N/P nutrient balance under phosphorus deficiency.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper treats NRT2.1 as a major direct target/output gene rather than the principal research gene; included because it is a core experimentally validated nutrient-metabolism gene in the Results.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrate uptake needed for nitrogen nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NAR2.1 expression is repressed by NIGT1 proteins, coordinating reduced nitrate uptake capacity
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRT2 accessory protein / nitrate assimilation-related partner protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate-inducible with time-dependent expression in roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: nitrate-inducible with time-dependent expression in roots
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course; ChIP for promoter binding by NIGT1.1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native NAR2.1 promoter analyzed by ChIP
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAR2.1 shows time-dependent nitrate induction similar to NRT2.1. 2. Its transcript level is elevated in nigt1 quadruple mutants and reduced in NIGT1.1 overexpressors. 3. NIGT1.1 binds the NAR2.1 promoter in vivo, indicating direct repression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be co-targeted with NRT2.1 to tune nitrate uptake systems.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced uptake may impair nutrient balance under phosphorus starvation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NAR2.1 is described as an accessory protein essential for NRT2.1 activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes hydroxylation step required for biosynthesis of trans-zeatin-type cytokinin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the target hormone class
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme for biosynthesis of tZ-type cytokinin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NIGT1-mediated repression of CYP735A2 dampens nitrate-induced accumulation of tZ-type cytokinin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate-responsive gene; downregulated in NIGT1.1 overexpressors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome-wide expression survey; RT-qPCR in NIGT1.1-OX; ChIP for promoter binding; cytokinin metabolite profiling in NLP/NIGT1 genetic backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CYP735A2 promoter analyzed by ChIP
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray/transcriptome survey of nitrate-responsive genes; GEO GSE100903 for genes downregulated in NIGT1.2 overexpressor
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP735A2 is identified as a likely shared target of NLP activation and NLP-NIGT1 repression. 2. NIGT1.1 binds its promoter in vivo and overexpression of NIGT1.1 lowers its expression. 3. Genetic alteration of NLP or NIGT1 activity changes nitrate-induced trans-zeatin accumulation, supporting physiological relevance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating CYP735A2 indirectly via NIGT1/NLP networks could tune nitrate-responsive cytokinin production and growth adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing cytokinin biosynthesis may broadly affect development and architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The gene is included because the paper functionally links its regulation to a nutrient-responsive hormone metabolite output.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root nitrate uptake system; seedlings for cytokinin measurements
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nitrate supports nitrogen nutrition; tZ-type cytokinin mediates nitrate-responsive growth signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HRS/HHO family, NIGT1 clade, GARP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: nitrate uptake and assimilation-associated transcription; indirect control of cytokinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly represses NRT2.1 and other nitrate-responsive genes, thereby reducing nitrate uptake and nitrate-induced tZ-type cytokinin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central negative regulator linking nitrate and phosphorus signaling; major repressor in the NIGT1-centered transcriptional cascade
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NRT2.1; NAR2.1; CYP735A2; HYH; NIGT1.1; NIGT1.2; NIGT1.3; NIGT1.4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses NRT2.1 promoter, NAR2.1, CYP735A2, HYH, and NIGT1-clade promoters
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: nitrate via NLPs; phosphorus starvation via PHR1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: negative feedback/autoregulation of NIGT1-clade genes; part of NLP-NIGT1 feedforward repression cascade
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protoplast co-transfection; EMSA; ChIP; overexpression lines; quadruple mutants; promoter mutagenesis; RT-qPCR; nitrate uptake assay; cytokinin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter in NIGT1.1-MYC overexpression line; 35S promoter in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate inducible; expressed in shoots and roots; time-dependent induction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: nitrate inducible; expressed in shoots and roots
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate treatment; nitrogen starvation; high nitrate; phosphorus starvation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome survey integrating published nitrate-responsive dataset and GEO GSE100903
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIGT1.1 directly binds two proximal GAATC motifs in the NRT2.1 promoter and represses its nitrate-induced expression. 2. It functions redundantly with other Arabidopsis NIGT1-clade members but is sufficient to decrease NRT2.1, NAR2.1 and CYP735A2 expression when overexpressed. 3. NIGT1.1 autorepresses NIGT1-clade promoters and is itself induced by nitrate through NLPs and by phosphorus starvation through PHR1, integrating N and P signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target to improve nitrogen-use efficiency or maintain uptake under P limitation by weakening repressor activity or editing specific promoter binding sites.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression causes severe growth defects including smaller shoots and shorter primary roots; loss of all four NIGT1s slightly reduces lateral root length and may disturb N/P homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study positions NIGT1.1 as the central experimentally validated regulator in the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N nutrition and nitrate-responsive cytokinin signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HRS/HHO family, NIGT1 clade, GARP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: nitrate-responsive transcription and indirect cytokinin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression represses a large set of nitrate-induced genes and alleviates nitrate-induced tZ-type cytokinin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: redundant NIGT1-clade repressor with genome-wide impact on nitrate-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NRT2.1-associated nitrate-responsive gene set; likely NIGT1-clade promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses NRT2.1 promoter in protoplast assays and downregulates many nitrate-induced genes when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in NLP-NIGT1 repression cascade and NIGT1 autoregulatory loop
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-transfection assays; overexpression transcriptome analysis; cytokinin profiling; complementation of nigt1 quadruple mutant with NIGT1.2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate inducible, expressed in shoots and roots; induction weaker than NIGT1.3/1.4
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genes downregulated in NIGT1.2 overexpressor, GEO GSE100903
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIGT1.2 functions as a transcriptional repressor of the NRT2.1 promoter. 2. Overexpression represses hundreds of nitrate-induced genes, revealing broad control of nitrate-responsive transcription. 3. Overexpression reduces nitrate-induced tZ-type cytokinin accumulation, while complementation with NIGT1.2 restores the quadruple mutant phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful node for modulating broad nitrate response networks, but likely requires precise tuning rather than constitutive expression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression suppresses nutrient-responsive transcription and may impair growth-related cytokinin responses.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NIGT1.2 promoter was not activated by NLP7 in the 1-kb promoter assay, suggesting missing distal cis-elements.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HRS/HHO family, NIGT1 clade, GARP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses NRT2.1 promoter and participates redundantly in control of nitrate uptake-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: redundant negative regulator in the NIGT1-clade controlling nitrate-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses NRT2.1 promoter; its own promoter is activated by NLP7 and repressed by NIGT1.1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: nitrate via NLPs; phosphorus starvation via PHR1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of NLP-NIGT1 feedforward repression and NIGT1 autoregulatory loop
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-transfection assays; RT-qPCR; promoter assays with NLP7/NIGT1.1; ChIP on NIGT1-clade promoters; mutant analysis within quadruple background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIGT1.3 is strongly induced by nitrate and its promoter is activated by NLP7. 2. It represses the NRT2.1 promoter similarly to other NIGT1 proteins. 3. Its promoter is also autorepressed by NIGT1.1 and activated by PHR1, placing it at the convergence of nitrate and phosphate signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be targeted with other NIGT1 paralogs to fine-tune root nitrate uptake responses.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional redundancy implies that single-gene editing may have limited effect.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression is primarily root-specific, differing from NIGT1.1 and NIGT1.2.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HRS/HHO family, NIGT1 clade, GARP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: nitrate-responsive transcription and nitrate uptake regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses the NRT2.1 promoter and contributes redundantly to downregulation of nitrate uptake-associated genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: redundant NIGT1-clade repressor; mutation showed relatively strong effect on NRT2.1 expression among paralogs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses NRT2.1 promoter; autoregulatory repression by NIGT1.1 likely occurs
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in NIGT1 autoregulatory loop; weaker PHR1 responsiveness than other NIGT1-clade genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-transfection assays; RT-qPCR; promoter analysis; quadruple mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strong nitrate induction; root-specific, relatively high in cortex and root hair-forming cells
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIGT1.4 is nitrate inducible and represses the NRT2.1 promoter. 2. It contributes redundantly with other NIGT1 genes to NRT2.1 suppression in roots. 3. Although induced by phosphorus starvation, its promoter shows weak or no clear activation by PHR1 in the tested assays compared with other NIGT1 genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could contribute to root cell-type specific engineering of nitrate uptake repression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely involved in root developmental responses under phosphorus deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously associated with primary root shortening under P-deficient conditions in cited studies; in this paper, PHR1 regulation appears weaker than for other NIGT1 genes.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: coordinates nutrient homeostasis under phosphorus deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: GARP-type transcription factor, master regulator of phosphorus-starvation response
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: integration of phosphorus starvation signaling with nitrate uptake regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly activates NIGT1-clade genes under phosphorus starvation, leading to repression of NRT2.1 and reduced nitrate uptake
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master upstream regulator linking phosphorus starvation to nitrate uptake repression via NIGT1 genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NIGT1.1; NIGT1.2; NIGT1.3; weak/unclear for NIGT1.4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NIGT1-clade promoters through P1BS and related motifs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphorus starvation; release from SPX inhibition as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phr1 phl1 mutant expression analysis; protoplast transactivation assays; promoter mutagenesis; ChIP with MYC-tagged PHR1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assay effector plasmid; overexpression line with MYC-tagged PHR1 for ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Referenced prior microarray evidence for phr1 phl1 effects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHR1 is required for full phosphorus-starvation induction of NIGT1-clade genes. 2. It directly binds and activates NIGT1 promoters in vivo and in protoplast assays. 3. Through the PHR1-NIGT1 cascade, P starvation downregulates NRT2.1 expression and nitrate uptake.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for engineering balanced N/P nutrition, though modifications must preserve phosphate-starvation adaptation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weakening PHR1-mediated repression of nitrate uptake could impair adaptation to phosphorus deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHR1 is a core regulatory gene in this study because it causally connects phosphorus starvation signaling to nutrient uptake transcription.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: primary nitrate signaling and downstream cytokinin response
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: primary nitrate signaling; activation of nitrate uptake/assimilation genes and NIGT1 repressors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly activates NRT2.1 and several NIGT1 promoters, but its outputs are antagonized by NIGT1 repressors; necessary for nitrate-induced tZ cytokinin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: primary nitrate-response transcription factor initiating the cascade later tempered by NIGT1 proteins
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NRT2.1; NIGT1.1; NIGT1.3; NIGT1.4; likely broader nitrate-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NRT2.1 and NIGT1-clade promoters in a nitrate-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedforward induction of NIGT1 repressors that subsequently repress NLP target genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-transfection assays; EMSA; promoter mutagenesis; transcriptome comparison in NLP6-SUPRD and nlp6 nlp7-1; cytokinin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis with NLP6-SUPRD lines; cytokinin profiling in nlp6 nlp7-1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP7 directly activates the NRT2.1 promoter via distinct cis-elements from NIGT1-binding sites. 2. It also activates several NIGT1 promoters, forming the NLP-NIGT1 transcriptional cascade. 3. Reduced NLP activity impairs nitrate-induced trans-zeatin accumulation, demonstrating physiological importance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance nitrate responsiveness, but must consider downstream feedback via NIGT1 and pleiotropy in nitrate signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess activation may provoke imbalance in nutrient-responsive transcription; active NLP proteins also undergo faster degradation according to discussion.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although previously known, NLP7 is experimentally central here because it defines the activating arm antagonized by NIGT1.
